x-enumeration-literals.name: Scheduled Repayment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payment made according to the original loan or credit plan schedule.

Comment:
Example: Monthly mortgage installment of €500 due on the 15th of each month.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Early Repayment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payment made before the scheduled due date, partially or in full, to reduce principal.

Comment:
Example: Borrower pays €5,000 toward a loan before the next scheduled installment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Repayment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payment that covers only a portion of the scheduled amount.

Comment:
Example: Monthly installment is €500, but the borrower pays €300.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Final Repayment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payment that completely settles the outstanding balance of the loan or credit plan.

Comment:
Example: Last installment of €10,000 paid to close a term loan.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Interest-Only Repayment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payment covering interest due without reducing principal.

Comment:
Example: €200 interest paid on a €10,000 loan; principal remains €10,000.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Penalty Repayment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Fee or Penalty Repayment: Payment made to settle fees or penalties associated with the credit plan.

Example: €25 late payment fee paid along with regular installment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover-Linked Repayment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Payment associated with a rollover arrangement, adjusting principal, interest, or new schedule.

Comment:
Example: Partial repayment of principal rolled into the new term after term deposit rollover.
